label: Actuators & Motors
description: Series elastic actuators with rare-earth permanent magnets. Each humanoid contains 28–44 precision actuators. Current unit cost $500–$2,000 per actuator; total actuator BOM $13,500–$40,000 per robot.
bottleneck: Rare earth neodymium-iron-boron magnets; export-controlled from primary supplier. Strain wave gears dominated by two suppliers (combined ~43% global share).
supplyRisk: Dependent on rare earth exports; 400–437% price spike risk on export control. Machine tool lead times for CNC gear-grinding machines: 18–24 weeks.
_note: Gear suppliers are fictional analogues to Nabtesco and Harmonic Drive SE. Source: ROBOT-summary.md.

label: Dextrous Hands
description: Multi-finger dextrous manipulation systems — the hardest mechanical problem in robotics. Requires >100 individual precision components per hand.
bottleneck: Precision machining at scale; limited qualified suppliers globally. No existing production line capable of manufacturing at humanoid fleet volumes.
supplyRisk: High precision requirements limit manufacturing base. New machining infrastructure takes 18–36 months to commission.

label: Computing & Sensors
description: Onboard inference chips, vision sensors, LIDAR, and IMUs for spatial awareness and real-time control.
bottleneck: Advanced semiconductor supply — same constrained supply chain as AI data centres. CoWoS packaging sold out through 2026.
supplyRisk: Dependent on the same advanced chip supply chain as AI data centres. Any chip shortage cascades directly to robot production.

label: Battery & Power
description: Current: lithium-ion, 2–4 hour operational life. Next-gen: solid-state (higher density, faster charge, no liquid electrolyte fire risk).
bottleneck: Solid-state batteries still pre-commodity as of 2026; production yield rates low
supplyRisk: Improving rapidly; solid-state expected at scale 2028–2030

label: Chassis & Structure
description: Aluminium and composite frame. Relatively commoditised. Lightweight design critical for power efficiency.

label: Total Robot Cost (2025–2026)
description: Prototype-to-near-commercial range. Early prototypes: $150,000–$500,000 (2023). Near-commercial: $30,000–$150,000 (2025–2026). Mass-market target: $15,000–$30,000. Commodity target: $8,000–$15,000 (2033+).
bottleneck: Actuator supply chain is primary cost and volume constraint
supplyRisk: Goldman Sachs projects 1.4M annual unit shipments by 2035, up from ~16,000 in 2025. <20 companies expected to achieve high-throughput production by 2028 (Gartner).
_note: Market estimates: Goldman Sachs $38B TAM by 2035 (revised from $6B — 6x upward revision). Morgan Stanley / ARK Invest project $1–$5T by 2050. Source: ROBOT-summary.md.
